strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, embedded, low-latency, and infrastructure systems — exceeds the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record: founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform running on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Recent AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows
concerns: No evidence of principal/director-level scope — career history is senior/staff IC delivery roles, not org-wide technical strategy or engineering leadership
No recognized AI expertise: no AI-related advanced degree, no shipped AI products or platforms of note, no AI publications or talks
No experience advising strategic customers or partners, or driving cloud product strategy — talks and workshops center on testing and GIS, not AI/cloud strategy

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and AI-built products
15+ years engineering experience with entrepreneurial background

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science and roughly two decades of engineering experience spanning Node/TypeScript product work, DLang low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby, and cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes). Founding GISCollective demonstrates the entrepreneurial builder profile the role prefers. However, the role is a director-level AI strategy position requiring recognized AI expertise, demonstrated AI products, and experience advising strategic customers and inspiring organizations — the résumé shows only months of AI-adjacent tooling work (MCP generators since April 2025), no advanced AI degree, no AI thought-leadership footprint (talks cover testing, web internals, and GIS), and no evidence of operating beyond senior/staff IC scope. The gap between a strong senior full-stack engineer and a principal AI strategist is too wide on the evidence presented.
